grants.name: National Technology Innovation Fund (NATIF)
grants.description: State financial fund supporting enterprises in scientific research, technology development, and technology transfer. NATIF provides preferential loans, loan interest support, and partial grant funding (up to 30% of total project cost) for R&D, technology innovation, and commercialization of new technologies. Established under Decree 95/2014/ND-CP, the fund targets SMEs undertaking technology innovation projects in priority sectors. Also provides loan guarantees to help technology enterprises access commercial bank financing.
grants.amount_note: Grants: up to 30% of project cost (typically VND 500M–10B). Preferential loans: up to 70% of project cost at below-market interest rates. Loan guarantees available. Maximum total support depends on project scale and NATIF council approval.
eligibility: Registered enterprise in Vietnam
Must be undertaking technology innovation or R&D project
Project in a priority technology sector
Must demonstrate financial capacity to co-finance (minimum 30% own capital)
Must have viable technology commercialization plan

grants.name: SME Development Fund
grants.agency: Ministry of Planning and Investment (MPI) / Vietnam Development Bank
grants.description: Established under Decree 39/2019/ND-CP (amended by Decree 80/2021/ND-CP), the SME Development Fund provides concessional loans and loan guarantees to SMEs, with priority given to innovative startups, SMEs participating in industrial clusters and value chains, and women-owned enterprises. Interest rates are set at 80% of commercial bank rates. Eligible uses include working capital, fixed asset investment, and technology upgrading. The fund operates through partner commercial banks across all 63 provinces.
grants.amount_note: Loans up to 70% of project investment cost, max VND 20B per project. Interest rate: 80% of commercial bank rates. Term: up to 7 years for fixed assets, up to 3 years for working capital. No collateral required for loans under VND 2B for innovative startups.
eligibility: Registered SME under Vietnam's SME Law (Decree 80/2021)
Must have a feasible business or investment plan
At least 20% own capital contribution
Priority: innovative startups, SMEs in value chains, women-owned businesses
Must not have non-performing loans at credit institutions
Must meet SME definition: ≤200 employees or ≤VND 100B annual revenue

grants.name: Corporate Income Tax Incentives for Priority Sectors
grants.agency: Ministry of Finance / General Department of Taxation
grants.description: Under the Law on Investment 2020 (Law No. 61/2020/QH14) and Decree 218/2013/ND-CP (as amended), enterprises investing in priority sectors and disadvantaged regions qualify for significant CIT incentives. Benefits include preferential CIT rates of 10-17% (vs. standard 20%), tax holidays of up to 4 years, and 50% tax reduction for up to 9 subsequent years. Priority sectors include high technology, IT, renewable energy, environmental protection, education, healthcare, agriculture, and large-scale manufacturing. Location-based incentives target extremely disadvantaged socioeconomic areas and economic zones. Additional incentives available for projects exceeding VND 6,000B investment.
grants.amount_note: Preferential CIT rate: 10% (15 years, high-tech zone); 15% (investment incentive sectors); 17% (certain encouraged sectors) vs. standard 20%. Tax holiday: 2-4 years. 50% reduction: 4-9 following years. No monetary cap — value scales with profit.
eligibility: New investment project in priority sectors or disadvantaged areas
Must satisfy minimum investment conditions per sector
Must be registered and licensed with MPI or provincial planning authority
Must separate accounting for incentivized activities if enterprise has multiple lines
Must comply with environmental, labor, and tax regulations

grants.name: Import Duty Exemption & VAT Incentives
grants.description: Enterprises in investment incentive sectors and zones receive import duty exemptions on fixed assets including machinery, equipment, specialized transport vehicles, and construction materials not produced domestically. VAT incentive schemes include VAT refunds for exported goods and services, and 0% VAT rate on exports. Raw materials imported for export production are exempt from import duties for up to 5 years or until products are exported. High-tech zones and economic zones receive additional customs facilitation and bonded warehouse privileges.
grants.amount_note: Import duty exemption: 100% on qualifying fixed assets not produced domestically. Raw materials for export production: duty-free for 5 years. VAT 0% on goods and services exported. No monetary cap — value depends on import value and export volume.
eligibility: Investment project in investment incentive sectors/areas
Imported goods must be for creating fixed assets of the project
Items must not be on the list of domestically producible goods (for duty exemption)
Export-oriented enterprises for raw material import incentives
Must be licensed investment project
Must comply with customs declaration and accounting requirements

grants.name: Provincial Investment Incentives
grants.description: Vietnam's 63 provinces offer varying incentive packages to attract investment. Common incentives include land rent exemptions (full exemption for the first 3-15 years), subsidized land clearance and site preparation, reduced land use fees, and support for worker training and recruitment. Provinces also offer streamlined administrative procedures, investment facilitation services, and infrastructure support (roads, electricity, water) in industrial parks and economic zones. Major investment destinations like Bac Ninh, Hai Phong, Binh Duong, Dong Nai, and Da Nang are known for aggressive incentive packages, especially for FDI and technology-driven enterprises. Provincial competitiveness varies; enterprises should compare PCI (Provincial Competitiveness Index) rankings.
grants.amount_note: Land rent exemption: 3-15 years depending on sector and location. Worker training support: VND 1-3M per worker. Land clearance costs subsidized. Infrastructure at industrial park boundaries often provided. No fixed cap — varies by province and project scale.
eligibility: New or expanding investment project
Must be located within the province offering incentives
Project in sectors prioritized by the province or national government
Must meet minimum investment capital thresholds (varies by province)
Must sign an MoU or investment agreement with the Provincial People's Committee
Foreign-invested enterprises must obtain Investment Registration Certificate (IRC)

grants.name: National Digital Transformation Program Support
grants.agency: Ministry of Information and Communications (MIC) / Ministry of Science and Technology
grants.description: Under Prime Minister's Decision 749/QD-TTg (National Digital Transformation Program to 2025, orientation to 2030), the government provides support for enterprises adopting digital technologies. Programs include the 'SMEdx' platform for SME digital transformation assessment, matching grants for digital solution adoption, and the 'Make in Vietnam' digital technology enterprise support. The program targets digital government, digital economy, and digital society development. Support measures include: subsidies for digital consulting services, co-funding for cloud/ERP/CRM adoption, and preferential loans for digital infrastructure investment. Technology enterprises registered as 'Make in Vietnam' qualify for additional incentives including R&D cost deductions and preferential access to government ICT procurement.
grants.amount_note: Digital consulting subsidy: up to VND 50M per SME. Digital solution adoption: up to VND 500M co-funding (50% of cost). 'Make in Vietnam' enterprises: additional tax incentives and procurement preferences. SMEdx platform: free assessment and recommendations.
eligibility: Registered enterprise in Vietnam (SMEs prioritized)
Must be undertaking digital transformation project
For 'Make in Vietnam': must develop digital technology products in Vietnam
Must use Vietnamese digital solutions where available
Must comply with cybersecurity and data protection regulations

grants.name: Export Credit & Export Promotion Support
grants.agency: Vietnam Development Bank (VDB) / Ministry of Industry and Trade (MOIT)
grants.description: Comprehensive export support system comprising export credits from Vietnam Development Bank (preferential loans for export contracts), export credit guarantees, and MOIT export promotion programs. Includes National Trade Promotion Program providing subsidies for Vietnamese enterprises to participate in international trade fairs, buyer-seller matching events, and market research missions. Post-investment interest rate support available for exporters in priority sectors. Export credit insurance is also available through government-supported schemes. Designed to help Vietnamese enterprises — especially SMEs — enter new export markets and expand international presence.
grants.amount_note: Export credits: up to 85% of export contract value at preferential rates. Trade promotion subsidy: VND 50-200M per enterprise for international trade fair participation. Market research support: up to VND 300M. Export credit guarantees up to VND 50B.
eligibility: Registered enterprise in Vietnam
Must have confirmed export contracts or demonstrated export potential
Priority: SMEs, producers of processed goods, technology products
Must be in good standing with tax and customs authorities
Export products must comply with destination market standards

grants.name: Science & Technology Development Fund (S&T Fund)
grants.agency: Ministry of Science and Technology (MOST) / Provincial DOSTs
grants.description: Vietnam's Science and Technology Development Fund (also known as VINIF for research or provincial S&T funds) provides grants and concessional loans for scientific research, technology innovation, and technology application projects. Enterprises can apply for funding to develop new products, improve processes, acquire or transfer technology, and build R&D capabilities. The fund covers project costs such as equipment, materials, personnel, IP protection, and technology transfer fees. Special emphasis on projects with commercial potential and those that contribute to national technology self-reliance. Provincial S&T funds operate independently and often focus on local economic priorities such as agricultural technology, aquaculture, or craft industry modernization.
grants.amount_note: Grants: up to 50% of project cost (VND 200M-5B depending on scope). Interest-free or low-interest loans for technology commercialization. Provincial funds typically smaller (VND 100M-1B) but more accessible for local enterprises.
eligibility: Registered enterprise, research institute, or university in Vietnam
Project must involve scientific research or technology innovation
Must have project proposal approved by S&T fund council
Must co-finance at least 50% of project cost (for grants)
Must demonstrate capacity to complete project within timeline

grants.name: Innovative Startup Ecosystem Support
grants.agency: Ministry of Science and Technology (MOST) / Ministry of Planning and Investment
grants.description: Under Decree 38/2018/ND-CP, the government supports an ecosystem for innovative startups through multiple mechanisms: the National Innovation Center (NIC) providing co-working spaces and incubation, the National Techfest annual competition with prize money and investor matching, and provincial startup support centers. Startups may access seed funding, R&D grants, and tax incentives. The Vietnam National Innovation and Startup Ecosystem Support Project (ISEV) funded by the World Bank provides additional support channels. Key components include matching grants for prototype development, subsidized access to labs and testing facilities, IP registration cost coverage, and mentorship programs linking startups with domestic and international experts.
grants.amount_note: Prototype development grants: VND 100M-500M. Techfest prizes: VND 200M-2B. Incubation support (NIC): subsidized office space, mentorship. IP registration: up to 80% cost reimbursement. Seed funding matching: varies by program.
eligibility: Registered innovative startup in Vietnam (≤5 years from establishment)
Must have innovative or technology-based business model
Must demonstrate growth or scalability potential
For NIC programs: must be selected through competitive process
Must have at least one Vietnamese founder (for national programs)
Priority: startups addressing national challenges (health, agriculture, climate)

grants.name: Clean Energy & Environmental Enterprise Support
grants.agency: Ministry of Natural Resources and Environment (MONRE) / Ministry of Industry and Trade (MOIT)
grants.description: Vietnam's commitment to net-zero emissions by 2050 drives a growing suite of green enterprise support programs. Includes the Vietnam Environmental Protection Fund (VEPF) providing concessional loans and grants for pollution control, waste treatment, and environmental remediation projects. For renewable energy, enterprises benefit from feed-in tariff support, tax incentives under the Investment Law (renewable energy is a priority sector), and land use incentives. The Green Credit program through the State Bank of Vietnam encourages commercial banks to offer preferential green lending rates for certified environmental projects. Additionally, enterprises adopting circular economy models may access technical assistance programs from international partners channeled through MONRE.
grants.amount_note: VEPF loans: up to 70% of project cost, max VND 20B at preferential rates (2.6-5.4% p.a.). Green credit: below-market rates from partner banks. Solar/wind FIT: varies by technology type and commissioning date. Tax holidays: up to 4 years for renewable energy projects.
eligibility: Registered enterprise in Vietnam
Project must be in environmental protection, clean energy, or circular economy
Must have environmental impact assessment approved
Must demonstrate measurable environmental benefits
For VEPF: must pass fund's investment appraisal
For renewable energy: must be registered in national power development plan
